codex:
MAXIMALITY: IMPROVE

The paper is not ready for D0.5. The following finite same-assumption corrections are required:

1. Fix the formal randomization assumption. `ass:uniform-tie` still states only \(U\sim\mathrm{Unif}(0,1)\). It must state that \(U\) is independent of \(D_n\) and every \((T_v,Y^v)\). `ass:independent-target` only makes the triple independent of \(D_n\); it does not give independence within that triple. The all-\(n\) elbow and digit-splitting repair currently invoke an assumption absent from the formal node.

2. Replace the stale displayed CDF lemma. `lem:certificate-cdf-modulus` still headlines the twice-too-large
   \[
   \delta+\frac{C\gamma}{\gamma-1}\epsilon^{\gamma-1}.
   \]
   Its statement—not merely its proof—must give
   \[
   |F_v-F_w|
   \le \min\!\left\{1,E(u-w),E(w-\ell),\frac12E(u-\ell)\right\}.
   \]
   Moreover, the same assumptions give a strictly tighter certificate constant than the current proof:
   \[
   E(u-\ell)
   \le \delta+C\!\left[\frac{\gamma}{\gamma-1}\epsilon^{\gamma-1}
                       -\delta\epsilon^\gamma\right],
   \]
   obtained from
   \[
   \delta P(u\le L)+E[u1\{u>L\}]
   =\delta+E[(u-\delta)1\{u>L\}],\qquad L=\epsilon^{-1}.
   \]
   Thus state the corresponding half-scaled bound, preferably with the still sharper envelope obtained by integrating \(\min\{1,Ct^{-\gamma}\}\). Synchronize every theorem, TL;DR field, consumer, and sharpness discussion to that displayed lemma.

3. Correct `ass:quantile-margin` prose. It still falsely says the condition “excludes atoms and flat gaps.” Replace it with the round-2 substance: it supplies mass across admissible symmetric neighborhoods, permits an atom at \(r_w\), may be vacuous on one side at boundary quantiles, and does not rule out a flat interval immediately to the right. Only stable upper-quantile inversion fails; the mathematical condition itself must not be described as anti-atomic.

4. Delete the two obsolete handle definitions. `def:tail-modulus-handle` and especially `def:tail-regret-handle` still prescribe unresolved two-law/Le Cam programs, directly contradicting `honest_scope`, which says they are retired. Either remove them or replace them with consumed summaries of the actual ordinary-cell swap, five-cell witness, closed randomized repair, and triangular sequence. No live “handle” may describe future work.

5. Remove unsupported audit-realism assertions from the formal assumptions. The justifications for candidate normalization, containment, the upper tail, and graded width still say that an audit “can certify,” “can estimate,” or that the premise is “realistic.” No audit-generation theorem is proved. Describe these solely as externally fixed conditional premises, consistent with the niche and honest-scope fields.

6. Regenerate dependency metadata mechanically. Several `used_by` arrays are not the inverse of `depends_on`/class membership and omit proved consumers, while other entries encode inconsistent indirect use. After deleting the handles and replacing the CDF statement, rebuild all `used_by`, `depends_on`, symbol references, and consumer fields from the final graph.

7. Correct and strengthen the literature metadata/positioning. The Qiu–Dobriban–Tchetgen Tchetgen JRSSB citation has the wrong pages: the published article is volume 85, pages 1680–1705, not 1007–1028. The related-work paragraph should distinguish its unknown-shift impossibility/asymptotic-PAC setting from this externally fixed exact-list setting, while retaining the existing no-superiority caveats. The known-ratio characterization of Tibshirani et al. and the distinction from doubly robust asymptotic calibration and length optimization are otherwise appropriate. See the [published Qiu et al. article](https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/85/5/1680/7225361), [Tibshirani et al.](https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.06019), and [Yang et al.](https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/86/4/943/7618755).

The candidate-wise marginal-coverage language, closed-centered-hull repair, \(\eta\ge1-\alpha\) every-\(n\) elbow, and universal-upper-bound/supremum-one formulation are otherwise correctly implemented.

codex:
MAXIMALITY: IMPROVE

Round-3 corrections are not globally applied. Required D0 directive:

1. Formalize randomizer independence. Change `ass:uniform-tie` in both TeX and core to state that \(U\sim\mathrm{Unif}(0,1)\) and is independent of \(D_n\) and every complete pair \((T_v,Y^v)\). Update `free_symbols`, symbol references, sampling metadata, and proof dependencies. The current `ass:independent-target` only makes \((T_v,Y^v,U)\) independent of \(D_n\); it does not make \(U\) independent of the target pair, so the weighted-rank and digit-splitting proofs currently invoke an unstated premise.

2. Replace the displayed CDF lemma, not merely its proof. `lem:certificate-cdf-modulus` still states the obsolete
   \[
   \delta+\frac{C\gamma}{\gamma-1}\epsilon^{\gamma-1}.
   \]
   Its formal statement must instead headline the candidate-specific normalization result
   \[
   \sup_r|F_v(r)-F_w(r)|
   \le \frac12E|v-w|
   \le \min\{1,E(u-w),E(w-\ell),\tfrac12E(u-\ell)\},
   \]
   followed by the strongest parameter-only envelope. Remove all “formerly displayed inequality” commentary.

3. Add the missing strictly sharper same-assumption tail bound. Since \(u\ge w\), \(Ew=1\), and
   \[
   Eu\le 1+\int_1^\infty\min\{1,Ct^{-\gamma}\}\,dt,
   \]
   one has
   \[
   E(u-w)=Eu-1\le H(C,\gamma),
   \]
   where
   \[
   H(C,\gamma)=
   \begin{cases}
   C/(\gamma-1),&C\le1,\\[2mm]
   \dfrac{\gamma}{\gamma-1}C^{1/\gamma}-1,&C>1.
   \end{cases}
   \]
   Therefore the currently displayed \(\min\{1,K-1,W/2\}\) is not maximal; replace it everywhere by at least
   \[
   \boxed{\min\{1,K-1,H(C,\gamma),W/2\}}.
   \]
   Synchronize the lemma, population theorem, TLDR, project fill, limitations, and sharpness language. Do not claim joint sharpness of this full minimum unless separately constructed.

4. Correct the quantile-margin assumption’s own justification. It still says the condition “excludes atoms and flat gaps,” directly contradicting the theorem and witness. Replace it with the synchronized statement that the condition supplies mass across admissible symmetric neighborhoods, permits an atom at \(r_w\), may be vacuous on one side at boundary quantiles, and permits a flat interval immediately to the right; hence it does not ensure stable upper-quantile inversion.

5. Remove unsupported audit-generation claims from the formal assumptions. The justifications for candidate normalization, containment, upper tail, and graded width still say an audit “can certify,” “can estimate,” or makes the assumptions “realistic.” State only that these are externally supplied conditional premises. No learning, estimation, or audit-certification theorem is proved.

6. Delete the two obsolete handles. `def:tail-modulus-handle` still prescribes a future two-law construction, and `def:tail-regret-handle` still prescribes an unresolved Le Cam/dyadic-shell program. This contradicts `honest_scope`, which says they are retired. Delete both nodes and all references, or replace them only with completed-proof summaries. Also change `R_n`’s role from `"open target"` to a resolved minimax functional.

7. Rebuild dependency metadata mechanically. Current `used_by` fields are not inverses of direct `depends_on`/class membership. For example, `prop:causal-change-of-measure` directly depends on candidate normalization, but that assumption’s `used_by` omits it; conversely intervention domination and bounded outcome claim uses by the CDF lemma that are absent from its direct dependency list. Regenerate all `used_by`, `depends_on`, membership edges, free-symbol references, and consumer fields from the final graph.

8. Correct the actual bibliography entry for Qiu–Dobriban–Tchetgen Tchetgen to JRSSB 85(5), 1680–1705, DOI `10.1093/jrsssb/qkad069`. The related-work prose is corrected, but both rendered and typed bibliographies still say 1007–1028. The positioning as unknown-shift finite-sample impossibility plus asymptotic/APAC methodology is accurate. [Oxford Academic](https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/85/5/1680/7225361)

9. Complete the closed-hull admissibility proof by explicitly establishing measurability of
   \(\bar h_0=\sup\{S(y):y\in J_0\}\), using the finite-step monotone p-value representation. Use Lebesgue measure when discussing the possibly empty/open \(J_0\), since the declared `len` functional currently applies only to closed intervals. The containment and zero-length empty-set replacement arguments otherwise remain valid.

Candidate-wise marginal coverage, the \(\eta\ge1-\alpha\) all-\(n\) elbow, eventual zero regret below it, the shell-gap population impossibility, and the universal-upper-bound/class-supremum-one formulation are substantively sound.

codex:
MAXIMALITY: IMPROVE

Most requested mutations landed: the formal randomizer assumption, \(H(C,\gamma)\) and \(W\) CDF envelope, handle deletion, resolved \(R_n\), corrected assumption descriptions, candidate-wise coverage, and both regret elbows. The Qiu citation and literature distinction are accurate: JRSSB 85(5), 1680–1705, DOI qkad069, addressing unknown-shift impossibility and APAC guarantees ([Qiu et al.](https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.06126)); the distinctions from known-ratio WCP and doubly robust asymptotic calibration are also faithful ([Tibshirani et al.](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2019/hash/8fb21ee7a2207526da55a679f0332de2-Abstract.html), [Yang et al.](https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/86/4/943/7618755)).

Exact D0 actions:

1. Complete the closed-hull measurability proof. The proof merely asserts that \(\bar J_0\) is measurable; it does not derive measurability of \(\bar h_0\) from the finite-step p-value representation. Define, for example,
   \[
   \bar h_0=\sup\bigl(\{S(y):y\in\mathbb Q\cap[0,1],\ p_{\rm rob}^{\alpha+q_n}(y)>\alpha+q_n\}\cup\{0\}\bigr),
   \]
   prove this countable supremum is measurable and equals the acceptance-set supremum using downward-closedness, then explicitly use one-dimensional Lebesgue measure to prove
   \(\lambda(J_0)=\lambda(\bar J_0)\), including the empty-set replacement by \(\{m\}\). Only afterward invoke `len` for the closed hull.

2. Actually rebuild reverse dependencies. The core is not the inverse of direct `depends_on`/class membership. Examples: `thm:marginal-regret-degeneracy` directly depends on `ass:iid-source`, `ass:compatible-target-law`, `ass:independent-target`, `ass:uniform-tie`, `ass:candidate-normalization`, and `ass:upper-tail`, but none of those assumptions’ `used_by` arrays includes that theorem. The witness lemmas are likewise missing from the causal, boundedness, normalization, containment, tail, width, and margin assumptions they directly cite. Normalize the direct-dependency convention across every statement and regenerate every assumption’s `used_by` mechanically.

3. Finish randomizer metadata synchronization. Replace the symbol/setup description of \(U\), which currently mentions only calibration data and target “draws,” by independence from the joint element \((D_n,T_v,Y^v)\) for every \(v\); add \(Y^v\) to its symbol references. Prefer the same joint-element wording in `ass:uniform-tie`, eliminating any ambiguity between separate and joint independence. Add the regret theorem to its regenerated `used_by`.

4. Remove the stale weaker CDF display from `thm:population-price-nonvanishing`. Its formal statement still headlines
   \[
   \min\{1,\delta/2+C\gamma\epsilon^{\gamma-1}/[2(\gamma-1)]\},
   \]
   despite the newly proved strictly stronger same-assumption envelope. Replace that theorem clause and corresponding proof summary by
   \[
   \sup_{v,r}|F_v(r)-F_w(r)|
   \le \min\{1,K-1,H(C,\gamma),W/2\}.

codex:
MAXIMALITY: IMPROVE

The main mathematical conclusions appear sound under the frozen finite normalized ratio-list regime, but the fresh TeX/core still has material consistency and rendering defects. Apply this finite directive:

1. Repair the dependency graph.

   - Add `ass:candidate-normalization` to `lem:certificate-cdf-modulus.depends_on`; its proof explicitly uses \(\mathbb Ev=\mathbb Ew=1\).
   - Regenerate every assumption’s `used_by` as the exact reverse of final definition membership and statement `depends_on`, including:
     - `lem:ordinary-cell-swap-sharpness`
     - `lem:five-cell-shell-gap-witness`
     - `lem:finite-list-heavy-tail-control`
     - `thm:population-length-frontier-degeneracy`
     - `thm:sample-regret-degeneracy`
   - In particular, the current arrays incorrectly omit the heavy-tail lemma and sample theorem from the sampling/tail assumptions and omit both witness lemmas from their causal, certificate, and margin assumptions.
   - Include `def:minimax-regret` under `ass:compatible-target-law`, since that definition expressly imposes that target law.

2. Make \(H(C,\gamma)\) and \(W\) operative in the population theorem.

   Insert into `thm:population-length-frontier-degeneracy`, before the length conclusion,
   \[
   \sup_{v\in V_\epsilon}\sup_{r\in[0,1]}
   |F_v(r)-F_w(r)|
   \le \min\{1,K-1,H(C,\gamma),W/2\},
   \]
   with \(H,W\) exactly as defined in `lem:certificate-cdf-modulus`. Retain the subsequent conclusion that this vanishing CDF bound does not imply a vanishing connected-length price.

3. Repair the length domain and state the closed-hull equality precisely.

   - Redefine
     \[
     \operatorname{len}(I):=\lambda_1(I)
     \quad\text{for every interval }I\subseteq[0,1],
     \]
     so open and half-open conformal intervals are covered, with \(\operatorname{len}([b,c])=c-b\).
   - In the sample-theorem proof, name the acceptance set \(J_0\) and its closed centered hull \(\bar J_0\), then explicitly prove
     \[
     \lambda_1(J_0)=\lambda_1(\bar J_0)
     =\operatorname{len}([m-h_0,m+h_0]\cap[0,1]).
     \]
     Explain separately that if \(J_0=\varnothing\), adjoining \(\{m\}\) changes neither Lebesgue measure nor coverage adversely.
   - This removes the present contradiction between “length is invoked only for the closed hull” and the theorem’s explicit use of \(\operatorname{len}(\widehat I_n)\).

4. Qualify the limitation-section inequality.

   Replace
   \[
   |\mathbb E[(v-w)H]|\le\cdots
   \]
   by “for every measurable \(H\) with \(0\le H\le1\).” Without that restriction, the displayed claim is false.

5. Repair the TeX/core escaping and verify compilation.

   - Preserve `\ne` and `\notin` literally through JSON decoding. They currently render as `e` and `otin`.
   - Encode aligned row breaks as `\\\\` in JSON so they decode to TeX `\\`.
   - Repair the generated occurrences `\sum_{k e j}` and `a otin H_n`.
   - Render the setup symbol inventory in math mode or a verbatim/description environment. The supplied file currently fails `pdflatex` at the unmathized `[0,1]^mathcal_A`, before reaching the residual escape corruptions.
   - Require an end-to-end successful TeX compile after regeneration.

6. Bibliography/positioning check.

   The substantive descriptions of Qiu–Dobriban–Tchetgen Tchetgen and Yang–Kuchibhotla–Tchetgen Tchetgen agree with the journal records, and Schröder et al. is indeed NeurIPS 2025 ([Qiu et al.](https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/85/5/1680/7225361), [Yang et al.](https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/86/4/943/7618755), [Schröder et al.](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/hash/02435e97d53c423a68622cb3c42403fd-Abstract-Conference.html)).
